name: Sentinel 2
area: Global coverage (coastal and inland waters included)
quality: High-resolution, multispectral imagery with 13 spectral bands
availability: Free and open access via Copernicus Dataspace Ecosystem
legal_restrictions: Open data policy under the Copernicus program
description: Sentinel-2 provides high-resolution optical imagery for land monitoring, vegetation analysis, water bodies, and disaster response, with a focus on agriculture and environmental monitoring.
variable_s: Sentinel-2 has 13 spectral bands: B1 (Coastal Aerosol - 443nm), B2 (Blue - 490nm), B3 (Green - 560nm), B4 (Red - 665nm), B5 (Red Edge 1 - 705nm), B6 (Red Edge 2 - 740nm), B7 (Red Edge 3 - 783nm), B8 (NIR - 842nm), B8A (Narrow NIR - 865nm), B9 (Water Vapor - 945nm), B10 (SWIR-Cirrus - 1375nm), B11 (SWIR 1 - 1610nm), B12 (SWIR 2 - 2190nm).
spatial_resolution: 10m (RGB, NIR), 20m (Red Edge, SWIR), 60m (Coastal aerosol, Water vapor)
api_protocol:
The Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem catalogue is a web service that can be queried through 4 REST protocols
  - OData
  - STAC
  - OpenSearch
  - Sentinel Hub Catalog API
limitations: Cloud cover can affect optical data; preprocessing needed for atmospheric correction; large datasets require storage and processing capacity
